title: recv_marker opcodes 173-176 — OTP 24+ selective receive optimization
purpose:
OTP 24+ introduced `recv_marker` opcodes (173-176) to optimize selective receive. These mark the mailbox scan position before a receive so subsequent receives can skip already-tested messages. Without these opcodes, any module compiled with OTP 24+ that uses receive fails to load with `unsupported opcode 173`.

The existing mailbox already has `save_pointer: usize` infrastructure from B-019. The recv_marker opcodes provide explicit control over this pointer from compiled code, rather than the implicit management in the current `wait`/`loop_rec` cycle.
task: Add opcode arity definitions, loader decode, Instruction variants, and dispatch handlers for opcodes 173-176. For the initial implementation, implement as functional stubs that interact with the existing save pointer — the basic selective receive optimization. Full marker-based optimization (multiple independent markers per process) is deferred.

requirements.title: Opcode arity and loader decode
requirements.spec: Add opcode arity definitions for opcodes 173-176 and decode them into dedicated Instruction variants.
acceptance: Opcode 174 (recv_marker_bind): arity 2, decoded to `Instruction::RecvMarkerBind { marker, label }`
Modules compiled with OTP 24+ that use receive load without errors
Test: load a .beam file containing recv_marker instructions — no load error

requirements.title: Dispatch wiring and handlers
requirements.spec: Wire all four recv_marker instructions into the dispatch table. Implement handlers that interact with the mailbox save pointer.
acceptance: `recv_marker_reserve`: stores current mailbox length as the marker value in the destination register (or sets save pointer)
`recv_marker_bind`: binds the marker to the receive label — for the stub, this is a no-op (the save pointer already tracks position)
`recv_marker_clear`: resets the save pointer to 0 (clear the optimization hint)
`recv_marker_use`: sets the save pointer to the marker value (skip already-scanned messages)
Existing selective receive tests continue to pass

requirements.title: File size consideration for messaging.rs
requirements.spec: messaging.rs is at 685 lines (well over 500-line guideline). Adding recv_marker handlers (~40 lines) pushes it further. The handlers should either go in a new `opcodes/recv.rs` submodule or messaging.rs should be noted for splitting.
acceptance: If handlers go in messaging.rs: explicit acknowledgment of file size, with a note that messaging.rs splitting is deferred
If handlers go in a new file: `opcodes/recv.rs` created, `pub mod recv;` added to opcodes mod.rs
Either approach: tests verify all four opcodes execute without error

requirements.title: Integration test with OTP 24+ compiled receive
requirements.spec: Test that a module compiled with OTP 24+ that uses selective receive loads and executes correctly with recv_marker instructions present.
acceptance: A compiled module using `receive Msg -> Msg after 0 -> timeout end` (which emits recv_marker in OTP 24+) loads and executes
The receive correctly receives a sent message
The timeout path works when no message is available
Test uses a real compiled .beam fixture

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement multiple independent recv markers per process — single save pointer is sufficient for correctness
SHALL NOT modify the existing wait/loop_rec/loop_rec_end receive cycle — recv_marker is additive
SHALL NOT break existing selective receive tests — the markers are an optimization layer on top
SHALL decode and dispatch without error — even if the handler is a no-op, the module must load and execute
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
